question: Using FY2025 Connectivity segment figures, calculate YoY growth in revenue, operating income, and Segment Adjusted EBITDA. What do the differential growth rates imply about operating leverage within Connectivity?
facts: All figures are from the retrospectively recast consolidated S-1.
The retrospectively recast consolidated S-1 includes xAI and X Holdings for all periods presented.
Connectivity segment revenue was $11,387M in FY2025.
Connectivity segment revenue was $7,599M in FY2024.
Connectivity segment revenue grew 49.8% YoY.
Connectivity segment operating income was $4,423M in FY2025.
Connectivity segment operating income was $2,006M in FY2024.
Connectivity segment operating income grew 120.5% YoY.
Connectivity segment Segment Adjusted EBITDA was $7,168M in FY2025.
Connectivity segment Segment Adjusted EBITDA was $3,849M in FY2024.
Connectivity segment Segment Adjusted EBITDA grew 86.2% YoY.
Connectivity segment operating income margin was 38.8% in FY2025.
Connectivity segment operating income margin was 26.4% in FY2024.
Connectivity segment operating income margin expanded by 12.4 pp.
Connectivity segment Segment Adjusted EBITDA margin was 62.9% in FY2025.
Connectivity segment Segment Adjusted EBITDA margin was 50.7% in FY2024.
Connectivity segment Segment Adjusted EBITDA margin expanded by 12.3 pp.
Incremental revenue was $3,788M.
Incremental operating income was $2,417M.
Incremental operating income margin was 63.8%.
Incremental Segment Adjusted EBITDA was $3,319M.
Incremental Segment Adjusted EBITDA margin was 87.6%.
Depreciation & Amortization was $2,376M in FY2025.
Depreciation & Amortization was $1,508M in FY2024.
Share-Based Compensation was $369M in FY2025.
Share-Based Compensation was $296M in FY2024.
Impairment was $0M in FY2025.
Impairment was $39M in FY2024.

question: Estimate implied monthly Starlink ARPU using Q1 2026 Connectivity revenue and disclosed subscriber counts. State all assumptions and identify what the filing’s subscriber and revenue definitions exclude from this ARPU estimate.
facts: Total Connectivity Revenue was $3,257 million in Q1 2026
Consumer Revenue was $2,148 million in Q1 2026
Enterprise & Government Revenue was $1,109 million in Q1 2026
Starlink Subscribers were 10.3 million in Q1 2026
Disclosed Starlink ARPU was $66/month in Q1 2026
Consolidated Products Revenue was $380 million in Q1 2026
Consolidated Services Revenue was $4,314 million in Q1 2026
Advertising revenue was $343.0M in Q1 2026
Advertising revenue is classified under the AI segment
Space segment revenue was $619M in Q1 2026
Space segment revenue includes Launch Services and Launch & Development
AI Solutions & Infrastructure revenue was $475M in Q1 2026
Starlink Subscribers were 8.9 million at year-end 2025
The filing marks 'Starlink Subscribers (in millions)' with footnote (1)
The filing marks 'Starlink ARPU (dollars per month)' with footnote (1)
The text of footnote (1) was not captured in the parsed document fragments
Connectivity revenue splits into Consumer revenue and Enterprise & Government revenue
The filing classifies revenue into Products and Services categories

question: What specific language does the filing use regarding timing of Starship commercial payload delivery, V3 satellite deployment, and V2 Mobile deployment? Compare the wording and assess what it suggests about execution and timing risk.
facts: The filing states Starship is expected to commence payload delivery to orbit in the second half of 2026.
The filing states Starship payload delivery to orbit is expected to follow additional flight tests.
The filing discloses 11 flight tests completed to date.
A 12th flight test is scheduled.
The filing does not specify how many additional flight tests are required.
The filing states delays or challenges in the Starship program have occurred in the past.

The filing states delays or challenges may occur in the future due to unforeseen technical challenges, supply chain disruptions, manufacturing difficulties, delays in launch and fueling infrastructure, regulatory hurdles, or the need for additional design modifications.
The filing states the company plans to demonstrate catching the upper stage.
The filing states the company plans to demonstrate in-orbit propellant transfer capabilities.
The filing states any significant delay in achieving key development milestones would impede the expansion of service offerings.
The filing states any significant delay in achieving key development milestones would defer anticipated revenue streams.
The filing states V3 satellites are designed to offer one Tbps of downlink capacity per satellite.
The filing states V3 satellite deployment is expected to commence in the second half of 2026 using Starship.
The filing states current operational rockets, including Falcon 9 and Falcon Heavy, are not capable of deploying V3 satellites and V2 Mobile satellites.
The filing states full reusability of Starship's upper stage is not required to deploy V3 satellites.
The filing states in-orbit refueling is not required for any of these LEO programs.
The filing states the core V3 satellite design is complete.

The filing states if the company is unable to successfully complete the development, testing, and deployment of Starship at scale in accordance with its anticipated schedule, or at all, its ability to execute its growth strategy such as the deployment of V3 satellites would be materially and adversely affected.
The filing states V2 Mobile satellite deployment is planned for 2027.

The filing states V2 Mobile satellites, combined with the EchoStar spectrum acquisition and optimized 5G protocols, are expected to increase capacity by orders of magnitude compared to the first-generation constellation.
The filing states Starship will be able to deploy approximately 50 mobile satellites per launch.
The filing states the Spectrum Acquisition Closing is expected to occur on or about November 30, 2027.
The filing states V2 Mobile satellites must be in orbit before the company can commence planned commercial Gen2 service in the United States.
The filing states the company must complete the acquisition of the relevant spectrum from EchoStar before it can commence planned commercial Gen2 service in the United States.
The FCC approved the spectrum transfer on May 12, 2026.
The filing states the company must secure the global right to use the spectrum acquired from EchoStar from a number of international telecommunications regulators.
The filing states there can be no assurance that such authorizations will be granted on acceptable terms, or at all.
The filing states some countries are considering ignoring or diminishing ITU priority as a mechanism to decide which operators are licensed.

The filing states the 5G NR-NTN bands for these same frequencies are not currently supported by RF front-end hardware for the provision of 5G-like service in any commercially available mobile devices.
The filing states the company has an operational coordination agreement with EchoStar.
The filing states the operational coordination agreement with EchoStar is expected to continue through 2026 and 2027.
